meta.title: Fertility Window Calculator — Expanded Window with LH Surge
meta.description: Calculate your fertility window with LH surge and daily conception probabilities. 10-day expanded window, peak days, and tips for trying to conceive. Free, no signup.

fertilityWindow.title: Fertility Window Calculator
fertilityWindow.description: Find your expanded 10-day fertility window with LH surge, peak days, and conception probability.
fertilityWindow.howItWorksText: The calculator estimates ovulation as LMP + cycle length − 14. Because sperm survive up to 5 days and the egg stays viable 12–24 hours, the expanded fertility window covers 10 days (7 days before to 2 days after ovulation). The LH surge starts 24–36 hours before ovulation. Daily conception probabilities are based on Wilcox et al. 1995.
fertilityWindow.source: Source: Wilcox AJ et al., NEJM 1995 — Timing of Sexual Intercourse in Relation to Ovulation

faq.q: What is the expanded fertility window?
faq.a: The classic window covers 6 days (5 before ovulation + the ovulation day). This calculator extends it to 10 days. Sperm occasionally survive 6–7 days, and ovulation timing varies. The expanded window gives more confidence when trying to conceive.

faq.q: What does the LH surge mean?
faq.a: Luteinizing hormone (LH) spikes 24–36 hours before ovulation. Over-the-counter LH urine tests detect this surge and are the most reliable predictor of imminent ovulation.

faq.q: Which day has the highest conception probability?
faq.a: Ovulation day (~33 %) and the day before (~31 %). Intercourse every 1–2 days during the peak window (day −2 to ovulation day) maximizes your chance.
For regular cycles (variation ≤ 2 days) the estimate hits in roughly 80 % of cases. For irregular cycles, PCOS, or breastfeeding, accuracy drops — combine with LH tests and basal body temperature.

faq.q: What if my cycle is longer or shorter than 28 days?
faq.a: The luteal phase (post-ovulation) is almost always 14 days. In a 35-day cycle ovulation is on day 21; in a 24-day cycle it is on day 10. The calculator adjusts automatically.

faq.q: Can I use this for contraception?
faq.a: No. Sympto-thermal methods (NFP) require basal body temperature and cervical mucus on top of calendar tracking. Calendar-only predictions have a Pearl Index of 9–40 and are not safe contraception.
